description: A chair sits between two makers, who revise the design as each sees what the other has built. 'wenola' is joint work on one undertaking or result, with the participants shaping it together. 'molawi' (cooperate) can divide compatible work among people; collaborators alter the same undertaking.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then move forward and unround into middle-height 'e'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper front teeth for voiced dental 'n' and let air pass through the nose into stressed middle-height back rounded 'o'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l' and finish with open central unrounded 'a'.
usage_notes: A plural subject may collaborate without an object; a counterpart can take comitative 'nua', and the shared undertaking can be stated directly. The event-noun rule supplies collaboration. Joint authorship or shaping belongs to 'wenola', while technical order among dependent tasks belongs to 'wephari' (coordinate).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Open designs and shared repairs can be collaborative when several people shape the result rather than merely complete assigned pieces. The word records that shared authorship.

semantic_domains.activity: working jointly on one undertaking or result
semantic_domains.creation: jointly making or revising a shared result
